title: Bridge Paper: [On the Evolutionary, Bio-Psychological Foundations of the Human-Animal Relationship].
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.
**Phase context:** nature connectedness, biophilia, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience, nature exposure

Paper: [On the Evolutionary, Bio-Psychological Foundations of the Human-Animal Relationship].
DOI: 10.13109/prkk.2023.72.8.666
Abstract: Ever since, people live in contact with nature and animals, even in relatively non-utilitarian ways. Erich Fromm and Edward Wilson termed this human universal "Biophilia". But why different species can live together in a social way, is explained by a "common social toolbox" of neural, psychological and physiologicalmechanisms, which evolved over phylogeny.Major components of this toolbox are found in the vertebrate brain, which evolved over the past 600 million years in a succession of key innov
